steps: Add 60.5 mg of 2 mM CAS dye to 50 mL Milli-Q H 2 O
Dissolve 73 mg of 10 mM CTAB (HDTMA) in 40 mL Milli-Q H 2 O
Make medium depending on the type of microorganism that you are trying to grow. For example, add ESAW-FE with casamino acids, peptone and agar for marine microorganisms.
Once both part I and part II are cooled (55ºC), mix the two solutions together and pour plates.
Bring solution to 1 L with Milli-Q H 2 O undetermined
Bring CAS solution to 100 mL final volume undetermined
Mix the piperazine acid solution slowly into the Fe-CAS*CTAB solution
Add 4.3 g anhydrous piperazine to the acid solution undetermined Piperazine anhydrous Contributed by users
Add 6.5 mL of 12 N HCl slowly to 25 mL Milli-Q H 2 O
Slowly add Fe-CAS mixture to CTAB solution while stirring
Mix 1.5 mL FeCl 3 -HCl stock (1 mM FeCl 3 dissolved in 10 mM HCl) with 7.5 mL 2 mM CAS dye stock Iron(III) chloride hexahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #44944
Take 6 mL 10 mM CTAB (HDTMA) stock and mix with 40 mL Milli-Q H 2 O CTAB (Hexadecyltrimethylamm onium bromide) BBI Biotech Catalog #CB0108-100g
Add enough 5-sulfosalicyclic acid to get 4 mM final concentration
Incubate plates according to the microorganism. Screen plates once colonies are grown: yellow halos = siderophore and blue = no siderophore
creator: Steven W Wilhelm
